Documentation on various Flask patterns, including application dispatching, caching, Celery integration, and more.


## Files
- **[appdispatch.rst](appdispatch.rst.md)**: Documentation on application dispatching in Flask, including techniques for combining and dispatching applications by subdomain or path.
- **[appfactories.rst](appfactories.rst.md)**: Documentation on using application factories in Flask for creating multiple app instances.
- **[caching.rst](caching.rst.md)**: Documentation on using Flask-Caching to improve application performance with caching techniques.
- **[celery.rst](celery.rst.md)**: Guide for integrating Celery with Flask to handle background tasks, including setup, task definition, and result retrieval.
- **[deferredcallbacks.rst](deferredcallbacks.rst.md)**: Documentation on using deferred request callbacks in Flask to modify responses after a request.
- **[favicon.rst](favicon.rst.md)**: Instructions for adding a favicon to a Flask application, including HTML link tags and routing.
- **[fileuploads.rst](fileuploads.rst.md)**: Documentation for handling file uploads in Flask, including setup, security, and configuration tips.
- **[flashing.rst](flashing.rst.md)**: Documentation for implementing message flashing in Flask applications, including examples and templates.
- **[index.rst](index.rst.md)**: Documentation of common patterns and interactions in Flask web applications.
- **[javascript.rst](javascript.rst.md)**: Documentation for using JavaScript with Flask to make dynamic web pages, including `fetch` API usage, template rendering, URL generation, and handling JSON data.
- **[jquery.rst](jquery.rst.md)**: Documentation note indicating that AJAX with jQuery is obsolete and refers to updated JavaScript patterns.
- **[lazyloading.rst](lazyloading.rst.md)**: Documentation on implementing lazy loading of views in Flask using centralized URL mapping and a LazyView class.
- **[methodoverrides.rst](methodoverrides.rst.md)**: Documentation for adding HTTP method overrides in Flask using middleware.
- **[mongoengine.rst](mongoengine.rst.md)**: Instructions for integrating MongoDB with Flask using MongoEngine and Flask-MongoEngine.
- **[packages.rst](packages.rst.md)**: Documentation on structuring large Flask applications as packages, including setup and module organization.
- **[requestchecksum.rst](requestchecksum.rst.md)**: Instructions for calculating and storing the SHA1 checksum of incoming request data in a WSGI environment.
- **[singlepageapplications.rst](singlepageapplications.rst.md)**: Instructions for serving Single-Page Applications with Flask by using static files and a catch-all endpoint.
- **[sqlalchemy.rst](sqlalchemy.rst.md)**: Documentation for integrating SQLAlchemy with Flask, including setup and usage examples.
- **[sqlite3.rst](sqlite3.rst.md)**: Documentation for using SQLite 3 with Flask, including connection management, querying, and schema initialization.
- **[streaming.rst](streaming.rst.md)**: Documentation for streaming large data in Flask using generators and context management.
- **[subclassing.rst](subclassing.rst.md)**: Instructions for subclassing the Flask class to customize request parameter handling.
- **[templateinheritance.rst](templateinheritance.rst.md)**: Documentation on using Jinja template inheritance to create base and child templates in Flask.
- **[urlprocessors.rst](urlprocessors.rst.md)**: Documentation on using URL processors in Flask for handling common URL parts like language codes.
- **[viewdecorators.rst](viewdecorators.rst.md)**: Documentation for creating and using custom view decorators in Flask, including examples for login, caching, templating, and endpoint decorators.
- **[wtforms.rst](wtforms.rst.md)**: Documentation for using WTForms in Flask applications, including form creation, validation, and template integration.
